metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'superseded by a sibling' mechanism concretely: specify what artifact/field records the supersede relationship (e.g. a signed attestation field, a structured `supersedes` reference on the closing ticket, or an explicit exclusion), so AC4 is implementable without executor invention.
findings.finding: The 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' branch of the `delivered-now` predicate (AC4) names no artifact, field, or mechanism that records the supersede relationship. An executor implementing AC4 must invent what 'superseded' means operationally — a new structured field on the child ticket, a naming convention, a human-authored note, or something else — without guidance from the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; Scope IN bullet 'in-epic sibling supersede'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Supersede protocol' sub-bullet under AC4: 'A child C is superseded by sibling S iff S carries a signed completion attestation whose `supersedes` array includes C's ticket ID; no unsigned/tag-based path is valid.' This keeps the supersede check verifiable by the same attestation-reading code path and avoids introducing a new gameable surface.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a compact schema table or enum block to AC2 that lists all valid values for each of the three dimensions (attribution, containment, layer), not just those referenced by the drop condition and fail-safes.
findings.finding: The value spaces for the three sub-question dimensions emitted by the Pass-2 sub-call (AC2) are only partially enumerated — the plan names values that appear in the drop condition ('delivered-now-child', 'limited', 'plan-semantics') and the fail-safe list but never provides the complete enum for each dimension. An executor must infer or invent the remaining values (e.g. what is the attribution value for a finding tied to an open child? to the container itself?).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the complete closed value vocabulary for each of `attribution`, `containment`, and `layer` (as named tuples mirroring `GRADED_BINARY`/`NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS`), and define the `{security,contract}` exclusion as a concrete criterion-id set or config list.
findings.finding: The enum value vocabularies for the three sub-questions (`attribution`, `containment`, `layer`) are never fully defined as closed sets. AC2 gives fail-safe examples inline ('insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep'), and AC3 implies specific values ('attribution=delivered-now-child', 'containment=limited', 'layer=plan-semantics'), but the complete legal value set for each field is never enumerated. Specifically: what are all valid `attribution` values when the finding does NOT belong to any delivered child? What are all valid `containment` values beyond `limited` and `spans-open`? Are there `layer` values beyond `plan-semantics` and `delivered-functionality`?
findings.location: AC lines 2–3 ({attribution, containment, layer} sub-questions and the Pass-3 drop predicate)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the containment enum value across 94fd (emitter) and 6533 (consumer) and the parent AC to a single canonical string (`limited-to-closed` preferred as the long form already used in 6533's worked example and 94fd's contract).
findings.finding: The `containment` enum value emitted by child 94fd (`limited-to-closed`) does not match the value child 6533 checks in its drop predicate AC item 1 (`limited`), creating a latent string-comparison bug where the drop condition `containment == 'limited'` would never fire against the sub-call output `'limited-to-closed'`.
findings.suggested_fix: Canonicalize to `limited-to-closed` everywhere: update 6533 AC item 1 to read `containment=limited-to-closed`, update parent AC#3 to match, and ensure 6533's pass3_decide implementation compares against the same string the sub-call emits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call: add a Pydantic model factory (mirroring `novelty_model`), register it as `plan_review_completion` in `register_contracts()`, and add a `plan_review_completion.md` reviewer prompt file with the three sub-question instructions.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call output contract — the structured-output schema, its Pydantic model factory, and the prompt file — is referenced throughout the plan but has no existence in the codebase. No model is defined, no contract is registered in `register_contracts()`, and no `.md` reviewer file exists (contrast: `plan_review_novelty.md` exists at `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md` and `novelty_model` is registered as `plan_review_novelty` in `passes.py:147`). An implementor cannot build the sub-call without these artifacts.
findings.location: AC line 2 ('A separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call'); ## What we build
findings.suggested_fix: Add `completion_model()` to `review_kernel/verify.py` (mirroring `novelty_model`), register it under `plan_review_completion` in `passes.py:register_contracts()`, and create `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md` with `outputs: plan_review_completion` and the ATTRIBUTION/CONTAINMENT/LAYER sub-question instructions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC4 (and What We Build) the verification/signing mechanism for the 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' path — either align it with the signed-attestation standard used for the completion-attestation path, or document why the sibling-supersede path is tamper-resistant despite being unsigned.
findings.finding: The Alternatives section rejects 'Tag- or progress-note-based suppression' explicitly because it is 'unsigned + gameable (prompt-injection surface into a security-sensitive gate)', yet AC4's second delivered-now path — 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' (in-epic sibling supersede) — carries no described signing, verification, or tamper-resistance mechanism. This is a cross-section contradiction: the security rationale used to reject unsigned suppression paths is not applied to the sibling-supersede path that is accepted.
findings.location: Alternatives considered (tag-suppression rejection) vs AC4 (delivered-now definition)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'sibling supersede is valid only when the sibling's own completion attestation covers the AC text of the superseded child (verified by attestation hash comparison), not by any unsigned metadata field' — or similar language that closes the gap between the security rationale in Alternatives and the AC4 definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2/AC3 whether ATTRIBUTION is (a) an LLM-emitted sub-answer from the Pass-2 sub-call, or (b) derived deterministically from the existing `_container_child` field set by pass1_container — if (b), explain whether ATTRIBUTION is even a sub-question or a pre-filter, and update the sub-call spec accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan claims Pass-3 is 'deterministic' but never resolves whether the ATTRIBUTION sub-answer (which closed child a finding belongs to) is computed deterministically from the ticket graph before the sub-call, or is an LLM-emitted output of the 'plan_review_completion' sub-call — a load-bearing distinction for the determinism invariant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an 'extend NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS' entry to Alternatives Considered, explaining why a parallel new sub-call is preferred over adding ATTRIBUTION/CONTAINMENT/LAYER as additional sub-questions to the existing score_novelty/novelty_model mechanism (or confirm it's compatible and reuse it).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a parallel new Pass-2 sub-call and Pass-3 predicate that structurally mirrors the existing novelty floor (ADR 0008 / score_novelty / rising_floor_drop / NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS) but never examines whether the three completion sub-questions could be added as additional entries to the existing NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS mechanism — the reuse-vs-parallel architectural choice is absent from the alternatives considered.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Alternatives Considered section, add: 'Extend NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS with completion sub-questions — REJECTED/CHOSEN because…', noting whether the fail-safe direction (insufficient → keep vs drop), the sub-call input (completion sub-call needs the child-delivery state; novelty sub-call needs prior findings), and the activation condition (container re-fire vs remediation re-review) are compatible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assign manifest-assembly ownership to one child (most naturally 6533's Pass-3 orchestration, since it already calls delivered_now and the sub-call) and add a corresponding AC item.
findings.finding: No child owns the step of assembling the 'delivered-children manifest' (iterating siblings, calling `delivered_now` per child, collecting their AC text) that child 94fd's sub-call must receive — 457a provides the predicate and 94fd consumes the result, but the assembly orchestration sits between them with no explicit owner.
findings.location: child 457a-e8da-92c4-450e (scope OUT: 'the Pass-2 wiring') and child 94fd-c930-9e69-497e (scope IN: receives manifest; scope OUT: 'the Pass-3 drop decision')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 6533's Scope IN: 'manifest assembly: iterating siblings, calling delivered_now per child, collecting AC text to feed plan_review_completion.' Add a corresponding AC item: 'pass3_decide (or its orchestrator) assembles the delivered-children manifest by calling delivered_now per sibling and passing only delivered children's AC text to the plan_review_completion sub-call.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deliverable to Scope IN for a new ADR (or equivalent spec doc) that codifies: the `plan_review_completion` sub-call interface, the {attribution, containment, layer} per-finding schema with valid values and fail-safe semantics, the `delivered-now` predicate definition, the evidence-gate flag and priority-floor config knobs (type, default, valid range), and the sidecar drops field shape.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple new architectural contracts — a `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call, a three-field {attribution, containment, layer} per-finding schema, a `delivered-now` predicate, a sidecar drops field, and two new config knobs (evidence-gate flag, priority floor) — but plans no ADR or dedicated spec document to codify these contracts, relying only on inline AC text and an informal 'decision comment + session log'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (in Scope or AC) to produce a new ADR capturing the completion-floor design: its purpose, the three sub-questions, the drop conditions, the security/contract exemptions, and the `delivered-now` predicate definition — mirroring how ADR 0008 documents the novelty floor.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pipeline stage — the 'completion floor' (a third floor beside the novelty floor ADR 0008 and drift-refresh ADR 0002) — with new suppression rules, a new Pass-2 sub-call, and a new `delivered-now` predicate, but does not state that a new ADR or design-doc update will be produced to capture this decision durably. AC line 1 records the decision in the ticket's own plan text and a 'completion session log', which is ephemeral ticket context, not a durable architectural record.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC line 1; ## What we build; ## Alternatives considered
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ## Scope (IN) or as a new AC line: 'A new ADR (ADR XXXX — completion floor) is created documenting: the three-sub-question decomposition, the Pass-3 drop conditions and their security/contract exemptions, the `delivered-now` predicate, and the config flags; referencing ADR 0008 and ADR 0002 as prior art.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the degrade behavior for a total `plan_review_completion` sub-call failure: define the safe-direction default (mirroring novelty's 'no answerable sub-answers → carryover' = keep all findings) and add it to the Pass-3 completion floor spec.
findings.finding: No fallback / degrade path is specified for total failure of the `plan_review_completion` sub-call itself (LLM error, structured-output parse failure, or timeout). The novelty sub-call's degrade is explicit: `novelty()` returns `0.0` (carryover → never dropped) when no answerable sub-answers exist, and `verify_findings` degrades to 'no verification' (INDETERMINATE) per the kernel. The plan says the completion sub-call mirrors the novelty sub-call, but the AC text says only that 'fail-safe values … all keep' — it does not specify what the completion floor does when the sub-call returns nothing at all.
findings.location: AC line 2 ('fail-safe values (insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep)'); ## What we build
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the novelty fail-safe: if the completion sub-call returns nothing or produces no parseable {attribution, containment, layer} for a finding, treat that finding as NOT suppressed (safe direction = keep). State this explicitly in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering constraint (or a CI check) that 77cf's calibration run is executed only after 94fd's prompt is merged and frozen, and that any subsequent prompt revision to 94fd triggers a mandatory re-calibration of the 77cf gold-set.
findings.finding: Child 77cf performs a LIVE LLM calibration run against the gold-set and records kappa scores, but implicitly depends on child 94fd's prompt wording being frozen before the calibration is executed; no ordering constraint or re-calibration trigger is specified if 94fd's prompt is revised after 77cf's calibration record is committed.
findings.location: child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757 (AC#3: calibration record) vs child 94fd-c930-9e69-497e (AC#4: wording is frozen)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 77cf's scope or AC: 'Calibration run is gated on 94fd being merged (prompt frozen); a CI rule invalidates the calibration record if plan_review_completion.md changes after the record is committed.' Alternatively, add a blocking dependency from 77cf to 94fd.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item to 77cf (or 6533) asserting that the completion floor introduces no change to review invalidation triggers and no modification to the close operation — ideally backed by a regression test or a documented negative-scope assertion in the test suite.
findings.finding: Parent success criterion 'No change to WHEN a review is invalidated (code-drift stays), no close-op change, no new attestation kind' has no child AC item or test explicitly verifying the close-op and fire-timing invariants are preserved; only the attestation-reuse aspect is partially covered by 457a.
findings.location: child 457a-e8da-92c4-450e (AC#3 covers attestation reuse only); no sibling covers close-op or fire-timing invariants
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 77cf AC: '[ ] A regression test (or explicit test-suite comment) asserts that enabling the completion floor does not alter which events trigger a review re-fire or modify the close-op pathway.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback statement to AC-3 or the 'What we build' section naming the evidence-gate flag as the back-out mechanism, and acknowledge that downstream Pass-3 output consumers (CI gates, dashboards) will see fewer findings when the floor is active.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a behavioral change to Pass-3 output (findings are now suppressed under the completion floor) but does not explicitly designate the evidence-gate flag as the rollback/back-out mechanism, nor does it state how consumers of Pass-3 output (CI gates, dashboards, downstream tooling) should handle the narrowed finding set.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC item 3; ## What we build
findings.suggested_fix: In AC-3, append: 'Rollback: setting evidence-gate=disabled restores pre-floor Pass-3 behavior (all findings surface); downstream consumers should tolerate variable finding counts or be documented as floor-aware.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
